title: FUNCTION — Belt and Pulley Mechanism

figures_detected.description: Belt and pulley mechanism diagram showing Low ratio (r2/r1 = 2.5037) and Overdrive ratio (r2/r1 = 0.4977) positions of primary (input) and secondary (output) pulleys with narrow/wide groove labels.
Steel belt cross-sectional view showing dimensions (22°, 24 (0.94), 9 (0.35), 14.5 (0.571), 2 (0.08), 0.2 (0.008) x 10), steel band length approx. 600 (23.62), and steel block count approx. 280.

content_blocks.text: The drives through a belt and pulley arrangement. This consists of a steel belt, an input (primary) pulley, and an output (secondary) pulley.
The pulley groove widths can be varied in the axial direction, giving continuous (stepless) speed changes from low (ratio 2.5037) to overdrive (ratio 0.4977).
Groove width is determined by hydraulic pressure acting upon a chamber in the sliding half of the pulley.

items.label: Speed ratio — Low (r2/r1)
items.condition: Primary pulley groove narrow, Secondary pulley groove wide

items.label: Speed ratio — Overdrive (r2/r1)
items.condition: Primary pulley groove wide, Secondary pulley groove narrow

content_blocks.text: The steel drive belt consists of 280 steel plates supported by two laminated steel bands.

When hydraulic pressure is applied to the output (secondary) pulley, the steel plates are forced upwards creating a tension at the bands. Power is therefore transmitted by compression of the steel blocks, with the laminated bands maintaining the necessary friction force.

Both input (primary) and output (secondary) pulleys consist of a fixed sheave and a sliding sheave. The slope of the sheave is 11 degrees. A hydraulic chamber is located at the rear of the sliding sheave. The sheave moves on a ball splined shaft.

model_notes: Applies to CVT (Continuously Variable Transmission) equipped Nissan Micra K11. Speed ratio range: low 2.5037 to overdrive 0.4977.
